purchases.merge(listings[['listing_id', 'created_at', 'expires_at']], on='listing_id', how='left')\nvalid_mask = (purch_with_listing['purchase_time'] >= purch_with_listing['created_at']) & \\\n             (purch_with_listing['purchase_time'] <= purch_with_listing['expires_at'])\nprint(f\"Valid purchases: {valid_mask.sum()} / {len(purchases)}\")\nprint(f\"Invalid purchases: cat_map[row['category_id']] = row['parent_category_id']\n\nprint(f\"\\nTop-level categories: {len([k for k,v in cat_map.items() if k==v])}\")\n\n# Join purchases with listing category and buyer city\npurch_full = purchases.merge(listings[['listing_id', 'category_id']], on='listing_id', how='left')\npurch_full = purch_full.merge(users[['user_id', 'city', 'user_type']], left_on='buyer_id', right_on='user_id', how='left')\n\n# Add top-level category\npurch_full['top_category_id'] = purch_full['category_id'].map(cat_map)\npurch_full['top_category_name'] = purch_full['top_category_id'].map(cat_name_map)\npurch_full['sub_category_name'] = purch_full['category_id'].map(cat_name_map)\n\nprint(f\"\\nPurchases by user type:\")\nprint(purch_full['user_type'].value_counts())\n\nprint(f\"\\nPurchases by top category:\")\nprint(purch_full['top_category_name'].value_counts())\n\nEOF\npython3 {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01EnqU8x1wNSanxpmPwHFb9C","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Jg4BtS6oRqh5svUUvTxPc4","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat << 'EOF' > /app/main_analysis.py\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy import stats\nimport glob, json\n\n# Load data\nusers = pd.read_csv('/app/user_001.csv')\ncategories = pd.read_csv('/app/category_001.csv')\nlistings = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('/app/listing_*.csv'))], ignore_index=True)\npurchases = pd.read_csv('/app/purchase_001.csv')\n\npurchases['purchase_time'] = pd.to_datetime(purchases['purchase_time'])\n\n# Category mappings\ncat_name_map = dict(zip(categories['category_id'], categories['category_name']))\ncat_parent_map = {}\nfor _, row in categories.iterrows():\n    if row['parent_category_id'] == 0:\n        cat_parent_map[row['category_id']] = row['category_id']\n    else:\n        cat_parent_map[row['category_id']] = row['parent_category_id']\n\n# Join purchases with listing category and buyer info\npurch_full = purchases.merge(listings[['listing_id', 'category_id']], on='listing_id', how='left')\npurch_full = purch_full.merge(users[['user_id', 'city', 'user_type']], left_on='buyer_id', right_on='user_id', how='left')\npurch_full['top_cat'] = purch_full['category_id'].map(cat_parent_map)\npurch_full['top_cat_name'] = purch_full['top_cat'].map(cat_name_map)\n\n# City user counts\ncity_users = users.groupby('city').size().reset_index(name='user_count')\ncity_type_counts = users.groupby(['city', 'user_type']).size().reset_index(name='type_count')\n\n# Define significant cities (non-negligible number of users)\n# Looking at distribution: 30 cities have >= 50 users\nMIN_USERS = 50\nsignificant_cities = city_users[city_users['user_count'] >= MIN_USERS]['city'].tolist()\nprint(f\"Significant cities (>= {MIN_USERS} users): {len(significant_cities)}\")\nprint(sorted(significant_cities))\n\n# Filter purchases to buyers in significant cities\npurch_sig = purch_full[purch_full['city'].isin(significant_cities)].copy()\n\n# Key analysis: for each city x category, compute purchases per user\n# Account for user type differences\n\n# Step 1: Calculate purchase rate per (user_type, top_category) across ALL significant cities\ntop_cats = purch_full['top_cat_name'].dropna().unique()\nuser_types = users['user_type'].unique()\n\n# Users in significant cities\nusers_sig = users[users['city'].isin(significant_cities)]\n\n# Global purchase rate per user_type per category (as purchases per user of that type)\nglobal_rates = {}\nfor utype in user_types:\n    n_users = (users_sig['user_type'] == utype).sum()\n    for cat in top_cats:\n        n_purchases = ((purch_sig['user_type'] == utype) & (purch_sig['top_cat_name'] == cat)).sum()\n        global_rates[(utype, cat)] = n_purchases / n_users if n_users > 0 else 0\n\nprint(\"\\nGlobal purchase rates per user type per category:\")\nfor utype in user_types:\n    print(f\"\\n{utype}:\")\n    for cat in sorted(top_cats):\n        print(f\"  {cat}: {global_rates[(utype, cat)]:.4f}\")\n\nEOF\npython3 /app/main_analysis.py","description":"Run main analysis - purchase rates by user type and {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01RAo618NnzGxHMrdeQG2drg","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01NSbBphW5soLMxGcuvET49J","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat << 'EOF' > /app/main_analysis2.py\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy import stats\nimport glob, json\n\n# Load data\nusers = pd.read_csv('/app/user_001.csv')\ncategories = pd.read_csv('/app/category_001.csv')\nlistings = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('/app/listing_*.csv'))], ignore_index=True)\npurchases = pd.read_csv('/app/purchase_001.csv')\npurchases['purchase_time'] = pd.to_datetime(purchases['purchase_time'])\n\n# Category mappings\ncat_name_map = dict(zip(categories['category_id'], categories['category_name']))\ncat_parent_map = {}\nfor _, row in categories.iterrows():\n    if row['parent_category_id'] == 0:\n        cat_parent_map[row['category_id']] = row['category_id']\n    else:\n        cat_parent_map[row['category_id']] = row['parent_category_id']\n\n# Join purchases with listing category and buyer info\npurch_full = purchases.merge(listings[['listing_id', 'category_id']], on='listing_id', how='left')\npurch_full = purch_full.merge(users[['user_id', 'city', 'user_type']], left_on='buyer_id', right_on='user_id', how='left')\npurch_full['top_cat'] = purch_full['category_id'].map(cat_parent_map)\npurch_full['top_cat_name'] = purch_full['top_cat'].map(cat_name_map)\n\n# Significant cities\ncity_users = users.groupby('city').size().reset_index(name='user_count')\nMIN_USERS = 50\nsignificant_cities = city_users[city_users['user_count'] >= MIN_USERS]['city'].tolist()\n\nusers_sig = users[users['city'].isin(significant_cities)]\npurch_sig = purch_full[purch_full['city'].isin(significant_cities)].copy()\n\nuser_types = users['user_type'].unique()\ntop_cats = sorted(purch_full['top_cat_name'].dropna().unique())\n\n# Global purchase rate per (user_type, category)\nglobal_rates = {}\nfor utype in user_types:\n    n_users = (users_sig['user_type'] == utype).sum()\n    for cat in top_cats:\n        n_purchases = ((purch_sig['user_type'] == utype) & (purch_sig['top_cat_name'] == cat)).sum()\n        global_rates[(utype, cat)] = n_purchases / n_users if n_users > 0 else 0\n\n# For each city, calculate expected and actual purchase counts per category\nresults = []\nfor city in significant_cities:\n    city_u = users_sig[users_sig['city'] == city]\n    city_p = purch_sig[purch_sig['city'] == city]\n    total_users = len(city_u)\n    \n    for cat in top_cats:\n        # Expected count: sum over user types of (count of that type * global rate)\n        expected = 0\n        for utype in user_types:\n            n_type = (city_u['user_type'] == utype).sum()\n            expected += n_type * global_rates[(utype, cat)]\n        \n        # Actual count\n        actual = ((city_p['top_cat_name'] == cat)).sum()\n        \n        results.append({\n            'city': city,\n            'category': cat,\n            'total_users': total_users,\n            'actual': actual,\n            'expected': expected,\n            'ratio': actual / expected if expected > 0 else 0\n        })\n\ndf = pd.DataFrame(results)\n\nprint(\"Results summary:\")\nprint(df.groupby('category')[['actual', 'expected', 'ratio']].mean().round(3))\n\n# Statistical test: Poisson test - is actual significantly less than expected?\n# Under null hypothesis, actual ~ Poisson(expected)\n# p-value for actual being too LOW (one-sided)\n\nfrom scipy.stats import poisson\n\ndef poisson_pvalue_low(actual, expected):\n    \"\"\"P-value for actual being too low (P(X <= actual) under Poisson(expected))\"\"\"\n    if expected <= 0:\n        return 1.0\n    return poisson.cdf(actual, expected)\n\ndf['pvalue'] = df.apply(lambda r: poisson_pvalue_low(r['actual'], r['expected']), axis=1)\ndf['deficit'] = df['expected'] - df['actual']\ndf['relative_deficit'] = (df['expected'] - df['actual']) / df['expected']\n\nprint(\"\\nTop city-category pairs with significantly LOW purchases:\")\nsig = df[(df['pvalue'] < 0.05) & (df['deficit'] > 0)].sort_values('pvalue')\nprint(sig[['city', 'category', 'actual', 'expected', 'ratio', 'pvalue', 'deficit']].to_string())\n\nEOF\npython3 /app/main_analysis2.py","description":"Statistical analysis of purchase rates by city and category"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1553,"cache_read_input_tokens":30232,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1553,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":25,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3caff44e-dd55-4bd2-be8f-b2e14cef7c9e","uuid":"9d97a759-5c77-475a-ac42-b0c0540aeeb8","request_id":"req_011CbfHuXTmQcMMF51z1ooP6"}

After applying this stricter cutoff, only a handful of city-category pairs remain significant: Bilbao-Fashion with its extremely low p-value, Burgos-Vehicles, Vigo-Fashion, and Granada showing the most compelling evidence of unexpected variability.\n\nBut I'm wondering if Bonferroni is too conservative here—FDR correction might be more appropriate for exploratory analysis since it's less stringent. More importantly, I need to refocus on what the problem is actually asking: identifying cities where transaction volume could be increased through targeted strategies, based on patterns observed in other cities for the same categories.\n\nSo I'm really looking for situations where a category performs well in some cities but underperforms in others relative to what we'd expect. The expected count represents what we'd predict given the user mix, and significant deviations flag cities with unexpectedly low demand that could benefit from marketing intervention.\n\nI should focus on top-level categories since they have more statistical power, then apply FDR correction and examine effect sizes to identify the most meaningful disparities.\n\nFor filtering results, I'll combine statistical significance (FDR-corrected p-value), a minimum absolute deficit threshold, and a relative threshold like actual being less than 70% of expected to ensure practical significance.\n\nLet me dig into the actual data to see which city-category pairs stand out most. {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_011kvh9ZFgPcdV23P7zSXdAk","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Vzka99SEExgzQjBWQFSMKi","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"cat << 'EOF' > /app/final_analysis.py\nimport pandas as pd\nimport numpy as np\nfrom scipy import stats\nfrom scipy.stats import poisson\nfrom statsmodels.stats.multitest import multipletests\nimport glob, json\n\n# Load data\nusers = pd.read_csv('/app/user_001.csv')\ncategories = pd.read_csv('/app/category_001.csv')\nlistings = pd.concat([pd.read_csv(f) for f in sorted(glob.glob('/app/listing_*.csv'))], ignore_index=True)\npurchases = pd.read_csv('/app/purchase_001.csv')\npurchases['purchase_time'] = pd.to_datetime(purchases['purchase_time'])\n\n# Category mappings\ncat_name_map = dict(zip(categories['category_id'], categories['category_name']))\ncat_parent_map = {}\nfor _, row in categories.iterrows():\n    if row['parent_category_id'] == 0:\n        cat_parent_map[row['category_id']] = row['category_id']\n    else:\n        cat_parent_map[row['category_id']] = row['parent_category_id']\n\n# Join purchases with listing category and buyer info\npurch_full = purchases.merge(listings[['listing_id', 'category_id']], on='listing_id', how='left')\npurch_full = purch_full.merge(users[['user_id', 'city', 'user_type']], left_on='buyer_id', right_on='user_id', how='left')\npurch_full['top_cat'] = purch_full['category_id'].map(cat_parent_map)\npurch_full['top_cat_name'] = purch_full['top_cat'].map(cat_name_map)\n\n# Significant cities\ncity_users = users.groupby('city').size().reset_index(name='user_count')\nMIN_USERS = 50\nsignificant_cities = sorted(city_users[city_users['user_count'] >= MIN_USERS]['city'].tolist())\n\nusers_sig = users[users['city'].isin(significant_cities)]\npurch_sig = purch_full[purch_full['city'].isin(significant_cities)].copy()\n\nuser_types = users['user_type'].unique()\ntop_cats = sorted(purch_full['top_cat_name'].dropna().unique())\n\n# Global purchase rate per (user_type, category)\nglobal_rates = {}\nfor utype in user_types:\n    n_users = (users_sig['user_type'] == utype).sum()\n    for cat in top_cats:\n        n_purchases = ((purch_sig['user_type'] == utype) & (purch_sig['top_cat_name'] == cat)).sum()\n        global_rates[(utype, cat)] = n_purchases / n_users if n_users > 0 else 0\n\n# Calculate expected vs actual for each city-category\nresults = []\nfor city in significant_cities:\n    city_u = users_sig[users_sig['city'] == city]\n    city_p = purch_sig[purch_sig['city'] == city]\n    \n    for cat in top_cats:\n        expected = sum(\n            (city_u['user_type'] == utype).sum() * global_rates[(utype, cat)]\n            for utype in user_types\n        )\n        actual = (city_p['top_cat_name'] == cat).sum()\n        \n        # One-sided Poisson p-value for \"too low\"\n        pval = poisson.cdf(actual, expected) if expected > 0 else 1.0\n        \n        results.append({\n            'city': city,\n            'category': cat,\n            'actual': actual,\n            'expected': expected,\n            'deficit': expected - actual,\n            'ratio': actual / expected if expected > 0 else 0,\n            'pvalue': pval\n        })\n\ndf = pd.DataFrame(results)\n\n# Apply FDR correction (Benjamini-Hochberg)\nreject, pvals_corrected, _, _ = multipletests(df['pvalue'], alpha=0.05, method='fdr_bh')\ndf['pvalue_adj'] = pvals_corrected\ndf['significant'] = reject & (df['actual'] < df['expected'])\n\nprint(\"=== Significant underperforming city-category pairs (FDR < 0.05) ===\")\nsig = df[df['significant']].sort_values('pvalue_adj')\nprint(sig[['city', 'category', 'actual', 'expected', 'ratio', 'pvalue', 'pvalue_adj', 'deficit']].to_string())\n\nprint(f\"\\nTotal significant: {df['significant'].sum()}\")\n\n# Build answer\nanswer = {}\nfor _, row in sig.iterrows():\n    city = row['city']\n    cat = row['category']\n    if city not in answer:\n        answer[city] = []\n    answer[city].append(cat)\n\n# Sort\nanswer = {city: sorted(cats) for city, cats in sorted(answer.items())}\n\nprint(\"\\n=== ANSWER ===\")\nprint(json.dumps(answer, indent=2))\n\nEOF\npython3 /app/final_analysis.py","description":"Final analysis with FDR correction"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4223,"cache_read_input_tokens":31785,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":4223,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":22,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3caff44e-dd55-4bd2-be8f-b2e14cef7c9e","uuid":"bece961e-50ed-453e-9ed6-3c9ca835d0ac","request_id":"req_011CbfHxCrfakSg9tNGGSXAv"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","id":"msg_01C41zpg3uyRK67UUC1qFAHU","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"text","text":"The answer has been written to `/app/answer.json`. Here's a summary of the methodology and findings:\n\n## Methodology\n\n1. **Significant cities**: Filtered to 30 cities with ≥ 50 users (non-negligible user base), covering cities like Madrid, Barcelona, Valencia down to Almeria (98 users).\n\n2. **User-type adjustment**: Computed global purchase rates per *(user type × top-level category)* across all significant cities. Business users buy ~10× less than regular users; verified users are between the two. Each city's **expected** purchase count per category was computed by weighting the global rate by the city's actual user-type mix — controlling for compositional differences.\n\n3. **Statistical test**: Used a one-sided Poisson test (actual count vs. expected count) for each of 240 city-category pairs, then applied **Benjamini-Hochberg FDR correction** at 5% to account for multiple comparisons.\n\n## Key Findings (26 significant underperforming pairs, 12 cities)\n\n| City | Underperforming categories | Most notable |\n|------|---------------------------|-------------|\n| Bilbao | Fashion, Family | Fashion at only 53% of expected (p ~ 2×10⁻¹⁰) |\n| Burgos | Fashion, Vehicles | Vehicles at 21% of expected |\n| Granada | Electronics, Fashion, Vehicles | 3 categories affected |\n| Leon | Electronics, Leisure, Vehicles | 3 categories affected |\n| Cordoba | Electronics, Fashion, Services | 3 categories affected |\n| Las Palmas | Fashion, Services, Vehicles | 3 categories affected |\n| Toledo | Electronics, Sports | Both well below expected |\n| Vigo | Electronics, Fashion | Fashion at 63% of expected |\n| Santander | Fashion, Home | — |\n| Salamanca | Leisure, Vehicles | — |\n| Almeria | Fashion | — |\n| Gijon | Home | — |\n\nFashion emerged as the most consistently underperforming category across northern/western Spanish cities (Bilbao, Vigo, Santander, Almeria, Granada, Burgos, Cordoba, Las Palmas)."}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"stop_details":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":232,"cache_read_input_tokens":44722,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":232,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"global"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3caff44e-dd55-4bd2-be8f-b2e14cef7c9e","uuid":"017bef9d-55d3-4bcd-9abf-5322535adde6","request_id":"req_011CbfJ7qxLzH67pDJ26Jife"}
